sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
LEFT moved the objects back to their original positions. The black L-shape and charcoal L-shape moved back 3 columns. The yellow L-shape stays fixed.
Now I understand:
- LEFT/RIGHT moves the black L-shape (with white dots) and charcoal L-shape together horizontally
- The yellow L-shape is stationary (target?)
- The right edge bar is a timer that counts down
The goal is likely to align the moving shapes with the stationary yellow L-shape. Let me try UP and DOWN to see if vertical movement is also possible, and figure out what the matching condition is.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try UP and DOWN to see vertical movement
action(['UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After UP:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 70 and n['color'] != 'B' or (n['pixels'] <= 70 and n['color'] in ('B', 'c', 'W', 'Y')):
        if n['pixels'] <= 70:
            print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
